content.problemStatement:
Chef needs a program to solve a simple task. The program will start with an array of integers X. After the execution it should hold values X[1] + X[2] + ... + X[i] at all positions 1 <= i <= N. However, he wants this done fast and efficiently. He needs your help to output a program which can be executed in parallel on N machines and will produce a correct result. Note that he needs a program for some fixed size of array 1 <= N <= 1000.


A program consists of a series of synchronized steps. Instructions within one step are executed in parallel on multiple (at most N) machines which all have access to the shared memory where the array is located. You should not make any assumptions about the order in which these parallel instructions will be completed, but you can be sure that all instructions in the current step will be completed before any other instruction from the next step. There is only one type of instruction and it has the form "a+b=c". It reads the values X[a], X[b] and writes their sum in X[c]. The program should contain at most 20 steps and the total number of instructions should not exceed 2000.
constraints: The program should contain at most 20 steps
The total number of instructions should not exceed 2000
content.inputFormat: First line contains a single integer N, representing the size of the array.
content.outputFormat: The output should contain the number of steps in the program, followed by the description of each step. Each step should include the number of instructions and the list of instructions.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The program starts with the array [a, b, c, d, e, f]. After the first step, the array becomes [a, a+b, c, d+e, e, f]. After the second step, the array becomes [a, a+b, a+b+c, d+e, d+e+f, f]. After the third step, the array becomes [a, a+b, a+b+c, a+b+c+d, a+b+c+d+e, a+b+c+d+e+f].

The program starts with the array [a, b, c, d, e, f, g, h, i, j]. After the first step, the array becomes [a, a+b, c, d+e, e, f, g+h, i, j]. After the second step, the array becomes [a, a+b, a+b+c, d+e, d+e+f, g+h, i+j, i, j]. After the third step, the array becomes [a, a+b, a+b+c, a+b+c+d, a+b+c+d+e, g+h, i+j, i+j, j]. After the fourth step, the array becomes [a, a+b, a+b+c, a+b+c+d, a+b+c+d+e, a+b+c+d+e+f, a+b+c+d+e+f+g, a+b+c+d+e+f+g+h, a+b+c+d+e+f+g+h+i].
The program starts with the array [a]. Since the array has only one element, no steps are required.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by using a parallel prefix sum algorithm. The idea is to divide the array into smaller chunks and compute the prefix sum for each chunk in parallel. The final result can be obtained by combining the prefix sums of all chunks.
solution.timeComplexity: O(log N) per test case, O(T log N) total. The time complexity is logarithmic because we are dividing the array into smaller chunks and computing the prefix sum for each chunk in parallel.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — the dominant structure is the input array.

code.Python3:
import sys
N = int(sys.stdin.readline())
steps = 0
while N > 1:
    steps += 1
    instructions = 0
    for i in range(1, N, 2):
        instructions += 1
        print(i - 1, '+', i, '=', i, end=' ')
    print()
    N //= 2
print(steps)
